entry.title: Re: S4/HANA Fiori - Attachments - Insert Link - ERROR: UR...
entry.content:
I had the same issue. I opened an incident and got the solution. Please check Note 2667053 - CX_HTTP_WHITELIST was raised - SAP for Me (for S/4HANA 2022 SP02 in my case). It depends on the release. You may also want to check 3290787 - How to maintain entries in transaction UCON_CHW - SAP for Me

After whitelisting the URL the error has gone.

Dear SAP Developer Advocates Team,

Following up on a recent discussion with Andre Fischer, we would like to formally request the organization of an SAP ABAP CodeJam one day prior to SAP Inside Track NRW 2026:
https://community.sap.com/t5/dortmund-blog-posts/announcement-sap-inside-track-nrw-be-part-of-nrw-s-biggest-sap-event-in/ba-p/14299356

SAP Inside Track NRW will take place on Saturday, June 13, 2026, at the premises of adesso. To complement the event, we propose hosting the SAP ABAP CodeJam on Friday, June 12, 2026.

UNIORG GmbH is pleased to provide the venue and local support for the CodeJam with the following details:

Date: June 12, 2026
Time: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM
Location: Lissaboner Allee 8, 44269 Dortmund, Germany
Capacity: Up to 25 participants

We believe this would be a great opportunity to bring the community together for a focused, hands-on learning experience right before SAP Inside Track NRW.

Please let us know if this date works for you and what further information you may need from our side to move forward with the planning.

We look forward to your feedback.

Best regards,
Holger Schäfer
UNIORG GmbH

entry.title: SII tip for Spain #8: New Field for Hydrocarbons from Tax Deposits - February 2026
entry.content:
The Spanish Tax Agency has introduced a new field in the Suministro Inmediato de Información (SII) system to support enhanced reporting requirements for hydrocarbon transactions. This update affects the Issued Invoices Record Book (Libro registro de Facturas expedidas) and is regulated under Order HAC/27/2026, modifying Order HFP/417/2017.

In this post, the details of the new SII field and the customizing behind will be explained.

 

Introduction

Spain's Immediate Supply of Information  (SII, Suministro Inmediateo de Información) continues to evolve to meet regulatory requirements and enhance tax transparency. The latest regulatory update introduces a specialized field to identify invoices documenting the delivery of fuel from Tax/Bonded warehouses to the extractor company provided that a VAT payment guarantee evidence (including advance payments) is provided, as defined in the section eleven of the annex of the Spanish VAT Law (LIVA).

This change was formally published on January 22, 2026, through Order HAC/27/2026, following a public hearing process that began in December 2025.. Additonally, the Tax Agency has also published the updated XSD schemas, available through the developer’s portal:

  • SuministroInformacion.xsd
  • RespuestaConsultaLR.xsd

 

Details of the new SII Field <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>

The Spanish Tax Agency has added a new optional field to the Issued Invoices Record Book within the SII XML structure. This field serves as an identifier to flag specific hydrocarbon transactions.

  • <RegistroLRfacturasEmitidas>
    • <FacturaExpedida>
      • <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>
      • <TipoDesglose>
        • <DesgloseFactura>
          • <Sujeta>
            • <Exenta>
            • …

This new Identifier can contain the following values:

  • “S” (Yes) - The invoice documents hydrocarbon delivery from tax/bonded warehouse after the relevant VAT payment evidence was produced by the extractor company.
  • “N” (No) or empty (default) - The invoice does not document such transactions

Technical hints

The former customizing activity “Assign Additional Attributes to Input Tax Codes” is renamed to “Assign Additional Attributes to Tax Codes”, and will now contain the information for incoming VAT (Capital Goods) and for outgoing VAT (Fuels from Deposits)

In case the tax code is marked as “Fuels extracted from deposits”, the new tag <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito> will be rendered in the XML as “S”. Otherwise it will not be rendered.

It is important to mention that since this tag is at the same level that the group <TipoDesglose>, it will refer to the full invoice: in case of invoices with several lines, it will only take one tax code marked as “Fuels extracted from deposits” to generate the new tag<HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>, even in the case that other tax codes are not relevant for fuels extractions.

XML example:

 

 

In case of Contract Accounting (FICA), it is also possible to populate this value via "Change Invoice Details" Business Add-In (BAdI) implementation

 

 

 

 

Timeline

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, expected by March 12, 2026
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and ECC: Check the following SAP notes:
    • SAP Note 3710148 - Electronic Documents for Spain SII HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito tag Legal Change: Proxy Objects
    • SAP Note 3710149 - Electronic Documents for Spain SII: HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito tag Legal Change is on Expert Review
    • SAP Note 3701288 - SII Spain - Contract Accounting: Inclusion of Tag HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito in Electronic Documents (FICA)

 

Related Links

SAP remains committed to supporting customers through these regulatory changes, providing timely updates and comprehensive documentation to ensure smooth transitions and continued compliance. For technical questions or implementation assistance, please contact SAP Support or consult the relevant SAP Notes and documentation:

  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in ERP

Disclaimer

This blog post is intended for informational purposes only and reflects a simplified configuration approach based on current legal and system understanding. It does not constitute legal or implementation advice. SAP system behavior may vary depending on version, scope, and customer-specific settings. For full compliance and implementation support, please consult your SAP implementation partner or legal advisor.

entry.title: Calculating IBP Technical Week “Key Figure Date” in S/4HANA Using CDS View
entry.content:
When extracting data from S/4HANA into SAP IBP, a standard calendar week may span across two months.
IBP splits such weeks into “technical weeks”, and many key figures require the first date of the technical week.
while working on custom IBP integration for transactional data (key fugures), I discovered that S/4HANA does not provide this date natively.
So I created a very simple CDS view to derive it using the I_CalendarDate standard view.
 
Example week:
  • 29-Apr-2024 → 03-May-2024
  • Crosses April → May
  • IBP interprets this as:
    • Technical Week A: 29–30 April → Key Figure Date: 29-Apr
    • Technical Week B: 01–03 May → Key Figure Date: 01-May

The CDS view below calculates this value automatically for any date.

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'IBP : Get Key Figure Date for any Date'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZI_IBP_Key_Figure_Date
  as select from I_CalendarDate
{
  key CalendarDate,

  @EndUserText.label:     'Key figure Date'
  @EndUserText.quickInfo: 'First day of the IBP Technical Week'
  case
    when cast( WeekDay as abap.int4 ) < cast( CalendarDay as abap.int4 )
    then dats_add_days( CalendarDate, -cast( WeekDay as abap.int4 ) + 1, 'INITIAL')
    else dats_add_days( CalendarDate, -cast( CalendarDay as abap.int4 ) + 1, 'INITIAL')
  end as KeyFigureDate
}

Result :

 

This CDS view can be used for :

  • Custom IBP extractors (CPI‑DS or RFC-based)
  • Time-based aggregation logic in Custom CDS views for IBP

Hope this will be helpful.

Regards,

Bharat Bajaj

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Warsaw, Poland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 23 April 2026 in Warsaw, Poland  from 12:00 - 18:00 GMT+1. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location: KMD Poland, Inflancka 4a, 00-189 Warszawa.

The instructor is Andre Fischer

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

The event is kindly being hosted by KMD Poland, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Mastering Scheduling Agreements in SAP S/4HANA: Highlights to Long-Term Procurement Excellence
entry.content:
Scheduling Agreements – SAP S/4HANA

Overview

A scheduling agreement is a long-term procurement arrangement between a company and its suppliers that establishes predetermined delivery dates, quantities, and conditions for materials within a specific time period. Scheduling agreements support continuous raw material supply, predictable demand patterns, and just-in-time delivery requirements while minimizing inventory costs.

In SAP S/4HANA, scheduling agreements support continuous raw material supply, predictable demand patterns, and just-in-time delivery requirements while minimizing inventory costs

Key Benefits:

  • Fiori Apps Integration: Streamlined user experience with "Manage Scheduling Agreements" app 
  • Real-time Analytics: Enhanced reporting capabilities with embedded analytics
  • Simplified Processing: Improved MRP integration and automated release creation 
  • Mobile Capabilities: On-the-go approval and monitoring functionality

Key Links:

SAP Best Practices: Scheduling Agreements in Procurement (Scope Item BMR)

SAP Help link: Procurement Using Scheduling Agreements

 

Types of Scheduling Agreements

1. With Release Documentation (LPA)

Purpose: Provides controlled communication of delivery schedules to suppliers with formal release documentation

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Enhanced release creation profiles with automated MRP integration
  • Real-time delivery schedule transmission capabilities
  • Improved audit trail and compliance tracking

Common Applications: Critical raw materials like wood chips, chemicals, and packaging materials where precise timing and documentation are essential

Key Functions:

  • Enables creation of Forecast (FRC) delivery schedules for medium to long-term planning
  • Supports Just-in-Time (JIT) delivery schedules for short-term requirements
  • Maintains complete audit trail of all communications sent to suppliers

2. Without Release Documentation (LP)

Purpose: Direct transmission of delivery schedules to suppliers without intermediate release steps

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Immediate schedule transmission capabilities
  • Simplified processing workflows in Fiori apps 
  • Enhanced supplier portal integration

Common Applications: Standard consumables and maintenance materials with stable demand patterns

Key Functions:

  • Immediate transmission of schedule changes to suppliers
  • Simplified process for routine materials procurement
  • Reduced administrative overhead for non-critical items

3. Standard Scheduling Agreement

Purpose: Combines flexible delivery scheduling with standardized procurement processes

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Integration with central contracts for pricing 
  • Plant-specific delivery requirements
  • Enhanced supplier collaboration tools

Common Applications: Multi-location operations requiring standardized processes with localized delivery

Key Functions:

  • Leverages master agreement pricing conditions
  • Enables location-specific delivery scheduling
  • Maintains consistency across multiple plants

 

Quota Arrangements

Quota arrangement in scheduling agreements allows paper companies to distribute procurement requirements among multiple suppliers based on predefined allocation percentages. This ensures supply security, competitive pricing, and risk mitigation by avoiding single-source dependencies for critical materials.

S/4HANA Enhancements:

  • Simplified Configuration: Streamlined quota arrangement setup through Fiori apps
  • Real-time Allocation: Dynamic quota calculation and distribution
  • Performance Integration: Supplier performance metrics influence quota allocation

Configuration Elements

  • Quota Base: Percentage or quantity-based allocation among qualified suppliers
  • Quota Period: Typically aligned with production planning cycles (monthly, quarterly)
  • Quota Allocation: Automatic distribution logic that considers supplier capacity, performance, and strategic relationships

 

Scaling in Scheduling Agreements

S/4HANA Scaling Features:

  • Automated Calculations: Real-time scale application during order processing
  • Flexible Scale Types: Multiple scaling methodologies supported
  • Integration with Contracts: Centralized scale management
  • Condition Management: Enhanced pricing condition handling

 

Just-in-Time (JIT) Integration

JIT Delivery Concepts

  • JIT Call-offs: Short-term delivery schedules (daily or hourly) that signal suppliers to start production and deliver materials precisely when needed 
  • Delivery Schedules: Frequent communication of requirements, typically weekly for forecast schedules and daily for JIT schedules
  • Kanban Integration: Direct connection with production planning to trigger material replenishment based on actual consumption

SAP S/4HANA JIT Enhancements:

  • Real-time Integration: Seamless connection with SAP PP and advanced planning systems
  • Mobile Capabilities: Supplier access via mobile devices and Fiori launchpad
  • Predictive Analytics: AI-driven demand forecasting integration
  • Supplier Collaboration: Enhanced portal integration with real-time visibility

JIT Configuration Parameters

  • Delivery Tolerance: Typically +5%/-0% for critical materials to prevent production disruptions
  • Lead Time: Varies by material type - chemicals (2-3 days), packaging (1 week), raw materials (2-4 weeks)
  • Safety Stock: Minimal buffer inventory maintained to support true JIT operations

 

Key Takeaways

  • SAP S/4HANA scheduling agreements transform procurement through enhanced automation, real-time analytics, and streamlined supplier collaboration, reducing administrative overhead while improving delivery performance
  • Successful implementation requires careful attention to document types (LPA vs LP), proper master data configuration, and alignment with MRP processes to maximize JIT delivery capabilities
  • The integration of Fiori apps, mobile functionality, and embedded analytics in S/4HANA elevates scheduling agreements from basic procurement tools to strategic supplier relationship enablers

entry.title: Re: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content:
Thanks @ruediger_karl for the information.
According to the SAP references, i followed these guides - 

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/multitenancy/#enable-multitenancy
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/developing-multitenant-applications-i...

Here there was no explicit mention of using Tenant Management Service instead of the Service Manager.

I think, Tenant Management Service is for creating HANA database tenants. CAP MTX multi-tenancy typically provisions HDI containers per tenant via Service Manager

Also, when i add cds add extensibility, cds adds service manager instance to manage the db (create tenant specific HDI containers).

entry.title: Re: Backorder Processing in aATP - SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello Nipun,

I am creating a very simple WIN, REDISTRIBUTE and LOSE strategy scenario. I used Configure BOP Segment and created 3 strategies using Delivery Priority 01, 02, 03 as Selection Conditions for Sales Documents in WIN, REDISTRIBUTE and LOSE strategy whereas Sort Conditions is Record Creation date with blank  Custom Sort Sequence. Now created a Variant using the Fiori App Configure BOP Variant. Now for a Material, I have total stock as 8 PC's. I created 1st Sales Order with 5 PC's and kept delivery Priority as 03 which got confirmed. Now created 2nd Sales Order with 3 PC's with Delivery Priority 02 and this also got confirmed. Now 3rd Order is created with 6 PC's with Delivery Priority 01 and this got confirmed with 0 PC's as there is no stock now. Now in Configure BOP Variant I pressed the Simulation button and clicked on Show Last Runs button which shows below result-
1st Sales Order : From 5 PC's => 0 PC's which is correct as this will follow LOSE Strategy (Delivery Priority - 03)

3rd Sales Order : From 0 PC's => 6 PC's this also is correct as this will follow WIN Strategy (Delivery Priority - 01)

2nd Sales Order : From 3 PC's => 0 PC's this is not correct as this will follow REDISTRIBUT Strategy and the qty should become from 3 PC's => 2 PC's (Delivery Priority - 02).
Total Stock 8 PC's should be consumed by 3rd Sales Order (6 PC's) and 2nd Sales Order (2 PC's).
Request you to please have a look on my scenario and let me know where I am doing the mistake ?
Note : If I look your screenshots, in that WIN is becoming 0 =>9, LOSE is becoming 8 => 0 and REDISTRIBUTE is becoming 4 => 3.

Indranil Chatterjee

My scenario is the following: using a BTP trial account, I am creating an application in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) and connecting it to an SAP S/4HANA system. In this application I also plan to call the OpenAI API.

Currently I am getting the error:

[error] - 502 > {
code: '502',
message: 'Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET'
}

This happens when I call the API API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV from an S/4HANA environment. The S/4 system is a cloud (RISE) environment from an account where I do not have admin access — only SAP GUI access — so I am using SAP Cloud Connector.

What I have already tried:

code: '502',
message: 'Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET'

entry.title: Request for standard production reporting apps
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

We would like to request your support to identify standard SAP Fiori reporting applications in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that provide simplified and user-friendly production reporting, especially for process manufacturing scenarios.

Currently, the COOISPI report is available in the system; however, business users find it complex and difficult to interpret, particularly for day-to-day operational analysis. For this reason, the users are looking for more intuitive reporting applications, similar in concept to the informative and analytical apps available in the QM module, but focused on production execution.

The main business requirements are to:

  • Easily visualize total quantities produced over a given period
  • Optionally compare total produced quantities vs Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs)
  • Access production information in a summary and user-friendly format, without having to navigate order by order

The focus is informational repor

}

entry.title: Re: (-1) DBM Transaction is auto rolled back by detecting...
entry.content:
Hello,

The error has come because there is an error in the Transaction Notification. The issue is that when using Report and Layout Manager, the TN error is not shown on the front-end. It simply gives the error you have mentioned (at least till FP2602).

My troubleshooting led me to check the TN on User Master Data (object_type = 12). My guess is that when you import a new report, each User Master Data record has some update. So, the TN with object_type 12 is run for every USERID. If any of the records gives an error, the entire transaction (addition of report) gets rolled back.

Instead of turning off the TN, I suggest you troubleshoot which TN is giving an error.

I have also raised an incident with SAP requesting the TN error to be shown on the front-end in System Messages Log.

entry.title: Re: Multiple FI documents from a single reconciliation key ?
entry.content:
Additional information.

If fields DFKKOP-XEIPH & DFKKOPK-XEIPH are checked in the FICA document, then separate line items are formed within a single FIGL document during forward posting.

If field DFKKKO-XEIBH is checked in the FICA document, then separate FIGL documents are created during forward posting with FICA document number in the reference field of the FIGL document.

entry.title: Re: Does ASE 16.1 (AIX) support TLS lower than TLSv1.2?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have TLS 1.3 in 16.1:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE/791c41982ee345a19c4ec4b774222c4f/d255581382d14bacb565f5f15364289a.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

TLS 1.2 should be the default in 16.1 as well:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE_SDK/b8eac28c172c4538b569c29e5fda69f9/46e769421ca64e408c218f0d52238f67.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

If you can always set a cipher or protocol specifically.

sp_ssladmin setcipher

sp_ssladmin setprotocol

1> sp_ssladmin sd
2> go
sp_ssladmin Usage: sp_ssladmin command [, option1 [, option2]]
sp_ssladmin commands:
sp_ssladmin 'addcert', 'certificatepath', 'password'
sp_ssladmin 'dropcert', 'certificatepath'
sp_ssladmin 'lscert'
sp_ssladmin 'refreshcert'
sp_ssladmin 'setcipher', { 'TLS1.3' | 'TLS1.2' | 'TLS1.0' | 'FIPS' | 'Strong' |
'Weak' | 'All' | quoted_list_of_ciphersuites }
sp_ssladmin 'lscipher'
sp_ssladmin 'setprotocol', { 'TLS1.3' | 'TLS1.2' | NULL }
sp_ssladmin 'lsprotocol'
sp_ssladmin 'help'
(return status = 0)

We can accept lower ciphers/protocol, but it isn't the default going forward.

Hope that helps,
Ryan

Hi Leo,

If there’s nothing in the ATP tree below that last line, then CO09 isn’t creating a batch‑level ATP segment for MIXTEST1. that's only possibilities I think , In that case, CO09 will only show the aggregated ATP value (the 972 ), and the BAPI will pull the ATP from any segment your checking rule resolves to.

To understand exactly which segment the BAPI is using, I will need to look at the availability check setup for this material/checking rule (OVZ9 / OPJJ). If you can share screen shot of this configuration, I might pinpoint the segment the BAPI is reading.

entry.title: Using IF DATEDIFF() and TODAY() returns no value
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I've been stuck for some time now trying to use today's date as a reference point in a data action. I want to create a logic that looks at a month, compares it to how far away it is from the current month, and then references a value based off that result. The referencing works, but not the date comparison. So far I've gotten the closest results to what I am looking for using this statement:

IF DATEDIFF([d/Date], TODAY(),"MONTH") <= 0

However, this statement appears to not work because [d/Date] and TODAY() are differently formatted, resulting in always giving the value of 0 and making it useless.

 

Does anybody have an idea on how I can use the current month as a base point for this calculation?

entry.content:
I have a set of APIs from a CAP application which are available behind the AppRouter. I want to access these APIs from an application which will be using PKCE to authenticate.

The identity provider for CAP application with the APIs is based on IAS authentication as a private client. The PKCE application is also based on IAS authentication but is a public client to use the PKCE authentication flow.

All of the existing SAP documentation regarding the token exchange process refers to the exchange with XSUAA involved but I am not using XSUAA and will only be using IAS for authentication.

I am attempting to use a 'Destination' with an authentication of type 'OAuth2UserTokenExchange':

When I initiate a call via the destination providing the PKCE token with the intent of accessing the CAP application APIs, I am receiving the error:

Error: The destination tried to provide authorization tokens but failed in all cases. This is most likely due to misconfiguration. 
Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Unable to fetch refresh token from the specified token service URL. Response was: Bad credentials

I have confirmed I have provided the correct client ID and Secret as extracted from the 'Secret' I created in Trust -> Application APIs for the registered client for the CAP application via IAS.

Question:

1. Is it feasible to perform a token exchange from IAS to IAS on different clients?

2. Is this destination configuration correct?

3. Is this the correct location to get the client ID and client secret?

entry.title: Re: C_S4CPR – Implementation Consultant: SAP S/4HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hi,

I tried to answer your questions by giving some general input about the exam.

  • How the exam is structured (knowledge vs practical parts)

The tasks are presented as business scenarios, and candidates must complete the tasks by creating the required documents/business objects in the system. To create the documents (practical part) correctly, the candidate must have the relevant knowledge.

  • The level of difficulty compared to the learning journey content

The exam level is comparable to the content of the learning journey. The candidate must complete the content, including exercises (ideally several times), and make themselves familiar with the processes and the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

  • The type of tasks or scenarios you encountered (process execution, configuration, troubleshooting, etc.)

The majority of the exam tasks are similar to those in the learning journey exercises. But there are also a few tasks from the demonstrations or the explanations in the content.

  • How much hands-on system familiarity is really required

All the tasks are carried out on the S/4HANA system, so the candidates must be very familiar with the system.

  • Any advice on what to focus on during preparation

Before taking the exam, candidates must become familiar with the procurement processes and be able to carry out the required tasks for each process. They must also complete the first topic in the learning journey, Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including the exercises.

Additionally, the following may help.

Prepare a cheat sheet listing all required apps and the corresponding business roles for sourcing and procurement tasks. For instance, to create purchase orders, use the Manage Purchase Orders app with the BR_PURCHASER standard role.

Create all the required business documents for all the procurement-relevant solution processes in the learning journey several times. Remember/Make note of needed/essential settings that must be applied to the business document. For instance, setting the required flags when creating a material/product for a specific procurement process. Knowing the required setting for a business document for the corresponding process is crucial. Make note of such settings as you read the content.

The scenarios may combine different solution processes; identify the additional settings required for the business objects in such scenarios. Detail the definition/description of the procurement solution process and the required tasks. In some questions, the name of the solution process is not given, and the candidates are expected to find it from the given business scenario. They have to create the appropriate document based on the given scenario.

  • How many tasks/questions are there?

There are 5 scenarios/questions, each with several (3-7) tasks.

Best Regards,

Muenevver

entry.title: Re: Using IF DATEDIFF() and TODAY() returns no value
entry.content:
@linus_collin23 

Assuming your date format is YYYYMM for e.g. 202501, I tested the below 

INTEGER @MONTHCOUNT

@MONTHCOUNT =DATEDIFF([d/Date] ,TODAY() , "MONTH"), This returns 13 as [d/Date] is smaller (202501) and TODAY is 202602

So in your case IF DATEDIFF([d/Date], TODAY(),"MONTH") <= 0, this will only evaluate TRUE if [d/Date] is bigger than current date so 202603 and beyond.

Below is the test with [d/date] as 202605 and it correctly evaluates to -3

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Understanding why SM20 Does Not Show All Security Audit Logs
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Security Audit Log (SAL) is a critical security component used to monitor and record security-relevant activities within SAP systems as depicted below,

 

 

Traditionally, Security Audit Logs are:

  • Configured using SM19

  • Reviewed using SM20

  • Stored in the file system

However, with the evolution of SAP S/4HANA, the Security Audit Log framework has undergone significant architectural changes.

This blog explains the common confusion scenario which I recently faced.

The Scenario

In an S/4HANA we configure the security audit logs by enabling the filters and setting the parameters we require in SM19. After activation, we review logs using SM20.

Observation

Only critical system-level logs, such as - System settings changes and Client settings changes were visible. Other configured events were not appearing in SM20, despite being properly enabled.

At first glance, this felt like some Configuration issue, Incomplete activation, Authorization problem or system malfunction. However, the actual cause was related to the new SAL architecture in S/4HANA.

Root Cause

In S/4HANA systems, SAP introduced new transactions:

  1. RSAU_CONFIG – for Security Audit Log configuration
  2. RSAU_READ_LOG – for reading audit logs

Architecture

Now-a-days, by default the Audit logs are stored in the database instead of the file system.

The relevant configuration can be found in:

  •   RSAU_CONFIG → Parameters → Recording Target

The recording parameter would be set to “Record in Database” to make the Logs to store in database tables.

Behaviour

SM20 can only display logs stored in the file system. It cannot read logs which are stored in the database. As a result, when the recording target is set to database, SM20 may appear to show no results — even though logging is functioning correctly.

Why Does SM20 Still Show Some Logs?

Certain critical events are classified as “permanently active events.” These events are still written to the file system by default. That is why SM20 still displays system-level and client-level configuration changes

SAP_BASIS Version Information

You can use RSAU_CONFIG and RSAU_READ_LOG in any S/4HANA release that includes:

SAP_BASIS 7.50 or higher

Starting from SAP_BASIS 755, the old Security Audit Log environment (originally accessed via SM19 and SM20) is switched off and cannot be used anymore and the system redirects us to the new t-codes

  • SM19 → It calls RSAU_CONFIG

  • SM20 → It calls RSAU_READ_LOG

Conclusion

If SM20 does not show all configured logs in S/4HANA:

  1. Check the Recording Target in RSAU_CONFIG
  2. If set to “Database,” use RSAU_READ_LOG instead of SM20
  3. Understand that SM20 reads only file-based logs

entry.title: How We Built a Scalable Mass Hiring Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

In many organizations using SAP SuccessFactors HCM, hiring doesn’t always happen at a steady pace. Sometimes it comes in waves — seasonal hiring, rapid expansion, new project launches — and suddenly HR teams are expected to onboard large numbers of employees in a very short time.

From what I’ve personally experienced, this is when traditional hiring processes start to feel overwhelming.

I’ve seen HR teams juggling spreadsheets, manually entering data, coordinating approvals over emails, and constantly double-checking records to avoid mistakes. For a few hires, this might be manageable. But when the numbers grow, the process becomes stressful and error-prone. Small inconsistencies in pre-hire records can cause issues later in onboarding. Delayed approvals slow everything down. And HR teams end up spending more time fixing data than focusing on people.

What really stood out to me was how exhausting and reactive the process became during high-volume hiring drives. Everyone was working hard — but the system wasn’t working smart.

At the same time, simply trying to “move faster” without structure creates new risks. Speed without governance can impact compliance, reporting accuracy, and overall data integrity. So the real challenge wasn’t just hiring faster — it was hiring smarter.

That’s where the idea for a more streamlined and intelligent solution began.

Our Approach

To address these challenges, we built the Mass and Swift Hiring Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

While working on this solution, my focus was simple: reduce manual effort without losing control. I wanted to create something that HR teams would actually enjoy using — something that feels familiar, intuitive, and aligned with how SuccessFactors already works.

The application provides a clean and structured interface where HR users can manage employee hiring, initiate onboarding for pre-hires, and handle contingent worker hiring in a guided and automated way. Instead of jumping between systems or manually validating data, the process becomes smooth and controlled.

One area I paid special attention to was former employee rehiring. In real-world scenarios, this can get complicated. If not handled correctly, it can disrupt employee history and create reporting inconsistencies. So the solution intelligently detects rehire cases and ensures the correct hiring flow is followed — preserving data integrity while simplifying the process for HR.

Most importantly, automation here doesn’t mean losing governance. Every hiring action still respects validations, approvals, audit requirements, and compliance standards. The difference is that the system now does the heavy lifting.

With this approach, organizations can handle bulk or time-sensitive hiring with much more confidence and far less operational stress.

Key Features and Benefits

1. User- Friendly Hiring Interface

  • SAP Fiori-based responsive UI.
  • Guided steps for data entry, validation, and submission.
  • Supports desktop and mobile access via SAP Build Work Zone.

 

2. Rapid Mass Hiring

  • Create and process multiple hires in a single operation.
  • Preserves and significantly reduces hiring cycle time for bulk recruitment.
  • Ideal for graduate hiring, seasonal hiring, and high-volume recruitment drives.

 

3. Rehire Detection Using Core Employee Data

  • The application performs an automatic rehire check using the candidate’s First Name, Last Name, and Date of Birth, National ID and other attributes.
  • If an existing record is found in SAP SuccessFactors HCM with an inactive status, the system recognizes the individual as a rehire and continues with the rehire process instead of creating a new hire.
  • This helps avoid duplicate employee records and ensures continuity of employee data.

4. Download Standard Hiring Template

  • Provides a predefined, SuccessFactors-aligned template for bulk hiring data entry.
  • Ensures users follow the correct format, mandatory fields, and data structure.
  • Can be easily shared with recruitment partners or external agencies.

 

5. Import Data

  • Allows HR users to import data using structured files (CSV / JSON / XML).
  • Eliminates manual data entry for large hiring volumes.
  • Also allows HR users to manually create a single record directly from the application UI.
  • Supports validation of uploaded data before submission to SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Handles errors gracefully by highlighting invalid or missing fields.

6. Data Validation with Error Highlighting and Logs

  • Performs real-time and pre-processing validation on imported hiring data before submission to SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Validates mandatory fields, data formats, and business rules aligned with SuccessFactors requirements.
  • Highlights validation errors clearly at row and field level, making it easy for users to identify and correct issues.
  • Prevents partial or incorrect data from being processed, ensuring data integrity.

 

7. Download Validation Logs

  • Provides the ability to download detailed error logs for failed records.
  • Error logs include clear reasons for failure (e.g., missing mandatory fields, invalid values).
  • Enables quick correction and re-upload of only failed records.

 

8. Download Successfully Imported Records

  • Successfully imported records can be downloadable.
  • Users can download the list of successfully imported records for confirmation, and audit.
  • Provides full transparency on which records were created successfully in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

9. Create New Onboardee from Scratch

  • Enables HR users to create a new onboardee directly from the application, without relying on pre-hire data.
  • Supports direct entry of onboardee information through a validated UI.
  • Ideal for walk-in hires, urgent hires, contractor conversions, or scenarios where pre-hire data is not available.
  • Ensures all mandatory SuccessFactors fields are captured before submission.
  • Seamlessly creates onboarding records in SAP SuccessFactors using secure OData APIs.

 

10. Simplified Contingent Worker Hiring

  • Enables quick and bulk creation of contingent workers alongside regular hires.
  • Minimizes manual intervention and repetitive HR tasks.
  • Ensures contingent worker data is validated and created consistently in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Business Value

  • The solution enables organizations to significantly reduce hiring cycle times during high-volume recruitment by streamlining data capture, validations, and record creation for employees and contingent workers.
  • Built-in validations ensure that only complete and consistent data is processed, reducing errors in pre-hire, employee and contingent records and ensuring alignment with SAP SuccessFactors HCM business rules.
  • By simplifying and automating repetitive hiring activities, HR teams can spend less time on transactional work and focus more on strategic workforce planning and talent engagement.
  • Detailed success and failure logs provide clear visibility into hiring outcomes, supporting audits, reconciliation activities, and operational reporting without additional manual effort.
  • The solution supports both full-time and contingent worker hiring through a single framework, allowing organizations to adapt quickly to changing workforce demands.

Conclusion

High-volume hiring can quickly turn into a pressure test for HR teams and systems. I’ve seen how manual processes, fragmented steps, and constant follow-ups can slow down even the most capable teams.

The Mass and Swift Hiring Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM helps remove that pressure by bringing structure, automation, and reliability into the process. It allows organizations to scale hiring without sacrificing data quality, compliance, or control.

For me, this solution is not just about speeding up recruitment. It’s about giving HR teams breathing space — allowing them to focus on people, strategy, and growth instead of repetitive operational tasks.

When hiring becomes both fast and reliable, organizations don’t just fill positions quickly — they build their workforce with confidence.

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Story Calculation based on Variable like Option
entry.content:
@siva_sapbw 

Create 3 sliders for capturing the 3 thresholds value. Create 3 Script Variables and bind these sliders to them.

 

Now you can use these Script variables in calculation definitions.

Finally create calculated dimension using Use Measure Based dimension to define the text values for each measures 

You can adjust the sliders to tweak the range values accordingly and hide it if required. You could also use an Input Text Box if you are not happy with sliders.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: A Practical Solution for Multiple Counter Maintenance Plans During SAP PM Data Migration
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

This blog explains how Multiple Counter Maintenance Plans can be uploaded in mass during data migration. We all know that standard tools like LTMC and LSMW is not suitable for Multiple Counter Plans. The upload program is created using a Function Module MPLAN_CREATE. The FM internally creates data in tables such as MPLA, MPOS, and MMPT. The main focus is that we developed in a way that it derives cycle values directly from the cycle set (strategy) and dynamically populating the cycle structure in this FM. This approach made the creation automatic of this complex maintenance plan upload and an alignment with configuration.

BUSINESS REQUIREMENT AND CHALLENGE

We had a business requirement which was to create multiple counter maintenance plans based on cycle sets. During the migration phase, we faced the issue that this requirement proved to be more complex than expected because there is no standard LTMC template available for uploading multiple counter maintenance plans, and LSMW was also not feasible, because it cannot be recorded if there is a scroll as the cycle data becomes difficult to manage when a strategy contains more than four cycle lines. Manual creation was not an option due to the volume of data and the complexity of the cycle definitions.

To address this limitation, we developed a Custom Upload Program using a standard Function Module MPLAN_CREATE. This blog describes how this FM was structured and, how cycle values were automatically derived from the cycle set and populated into the cycle structure of the FM.

MAIN STRUCTURES USED

This Function Module contains the following structures, and we used those structures to develop the upload program. 

  • HEADER – Maintenance plan header
  • ITEM – Maintenance plan items
  • CYCLE – Cycle definition
  • RETURN – Messages

WHY MPLAN_CREATE?

We opted for MPLAN_CREATE because this FM provides full control over Maintenance plan header, Maintenance plan items and Cycle definitions. Instead of hardcoding the cycles, we wanted the system to always read cycle values directly from IP11Z. That way, the upload program would remain stable even if cycle sets change in the future.

OVERALL DESIGN IDEA

The program follows a simple logical flow. That is 

  • First, the maintenance plan header is prepared.
  • Next, the maintenance plan items are prepared.
  • Finally, the cycle data is derived from the cycle set and filled into the Function module cycle table.

INPUT PARAMETERS FOR HEADER

The header structure defines how the plan behaves. We defined only the required scheduling-related fields.

Key fields used in our program:

  • WPTXT – Maintenance plan text
  • ABRHO – Scheduling period
  • MPTYP – Maintenance plan category
  • HUNIT – Unit for Scheduling Period
  • PLAN_SORT – Sort field for maintenance plans

INPUT PARAMETERS FOR MAINTENANCE PLAN ITEMS 

For every maintenance plan there are multiple items linked to the same equipment, but with different task list counters. For every item, we filled the item number, equipment, task list type, task list group, and group counter. Order type, maintenance activity type, and priority were also passed.

One fascinating part here was the work center. The FM does not accept the work center name directly. It accepts the Object ID (OBJID). So, we derived it dynamically by passing the work center and plant into table CRHD and fetching the corresponding OBJID. This value was then assigned to the item structure. The below fields are defined in the item

  • WPPOS – Item number
  • PSTXT – Item text
  • EQUNR – Equipment
  • PLNTY, PLNNR, PLNAL – Task list assignment
  • IWERK – Plant
  • AUART – Order type
  • ILART – Maintenance activity type
  • PRIOK – Priority

WORK CENTER DERIVATION

The work center (GEWRK) cannot be passed directly by its name. So, the work center required by the FM was derived dynamically by using the below logic,

  • Input: Pass Work center (CRHD-ARBL) and Plant (CRHD-WERKS)
  • Output: Fetch the Object ID (CRHD-OBJID)

This OBJID was passed to the MPLAN_CREATE item structure.

READ AND INPUT THE CYCLE SET (STRATEGY)

Our upload program was developed in a way that cycle set is automatically fetched instead of entering cycle values manually. We passed the Cycle set name to table T351P. This table stores the parameters of each strategy, including the cycle values and units.

Input: Cycle Set Name which was created using IP11Z

Table used: T351P (Maintenance Packages)

Data Fetched from T351P

  • ZAEHL – Cycle number
  • ZYKZT – Cycle value
  • ZEIEH – Unit of measure
  • KTEX1 – Short text

By doing this, we ensured that the program always uses the same cycle definition as maintained in IP11Z. For every line found in T351P, one line was created in the MPLAN_CREATE cycle table CYCLE.

The fields were filled as follows:

  • The cycle number was taken from the cycle set sequence.
  • The cycle value was taken from the cycle definition in the cycle set.
  • The unit of measure was taken from the cycle set unit.
  • The cycle text was taken from the cycle set short text.

This mapping paved us to create multiple cycles for the same maintenance plan without manual entry.

Fields populated:

  • NUMBER → From T351P-ZAEHL
  • ZYKL1→ From T351P-KTEX1(fetch the first word, as the value is entered in the first word of the text in cycle set)
  • ZEIEH → From T351P-ZEIEH
  • PAK_TEXT → From T351P-KTEX1

COUNTER POINT DETERMINATION

As this was a counter-based maintenance plan, we had to determine the counter point dynamically. The equipment number was first passed into EQUI table to fetch the object number. That object number was then passed into IMPTT table to fetch the counter point. We selected the counter by filtering the characteristics name which represented the operating hours. This counter point was then assigned to the relevant cycle entries in CYCLE. This step ensured that the cycle was linked to the correct measurement counter of the equipment.

  1. Pass EQUNR → table EQUI → Fetch OBJNR
  2. Pass OBJNR → table IMPTT → Fetch POINT → Filter where ATINN = 'HOURS'

This POINT is passed into CYCLE-POINT.

SEQUENCE AND REPETITION LOGIC

To support multiple counter groups, we used:

  • CYCLESEQIND to define the cycle group
  • SETREPEATIND to indicate repetition

OUTCOME

The outcome of this approach is, 

  • Multiple counter maintenance plans were created automatically
  • Cycle data was always aligned with strategy configuration
  • No dependency on LTMC or LSMW was required
  • Manual creation effort was eliminated

The solution proved to be stable, reusable, and fully controlled.

CONCLUSION

MPLAN_CREATE becomes a practical and powerful alternative when standard migration tools do not support complex PM scenarios such as Multiple Counter Maintenance Plans with cycle sets. By deriving cycle values from the table T351P and counter points from the table IMPTT, we were able to automate the creation of upload program for Multiple Counter Maintenance Plan, while keeping the logic aligned with SAP configuration. This approach helped us to bridge the gap between functional configuration and technical automation in a clean and maintainable way.
